findings.title: Sharp rise in sovereign bond yields across US, Europe, Japan
findings.summary: Yields on 10-year bonds from US, Western Europe, and Japan surged in recent weeks amid inflation fears, policy tightening concerns, and fiscal deficit worries. China bonds bucked trend with falling yields. This signals heightened inflation expectations pressuring fixed income globally.
findings.claim_excerpt: sharp increase in the yields on bonds issued by many major countries including the United States, those of Western Europe, and Japan.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term trend from sustained inflation and policy shift expectations over weeks/months.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Bond selloff reflects investor wariness on inflation-fiscal mix, potential for tighter policy paths.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Direct data from comprehensive weekly economic update covering multiple regions.

sources_cited.excerpt: sharp increase in the yields on bonds issued by many major countries including US, Western Europe, Japan

search_queries_run: sovereign bond yields US Europe Japan
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Single detailed source but comprehensive coverage of global yields justifies High; cross-verified with known market trends.

findings.title: Crude oil prices soar on Middle East conflict escalation
findings.summary: Oil prices surged as investors priced in prolonged Middle East tensions. Equity markets dipped modestly while bond yields rose slightly. Energy sector volatility intensifies amid existing tariff and policy uncertainties.
findings.claim_excerpt: Crude oil prices soared as investors worried about the potential duration and intensity of the conflict in the Middle East.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic reaction to geopolitical event, noise unless sustained 3+ weeks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Geopolitical risk premium in oil adds inflationary pressure, but TS unless persistent.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from Confirmed: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Explicit market reaction tied to named event in primary economic update.

findings.title: US services PMI contracts to 49.8 amid price pressures
findings.summary: US services PMI fell to 49.8 in March, first contraction since Jan 2023, due to rising prices and uncertainty from Middle East war and tariffs. S&P notes economy buckling under strain. Signals softening in tech/services sector.
findings.claim_excerpt: PMI for the US services industry fell from 51.7 in February to 49.8 in March... buckling under the strain of rising prices and intensifying uncertainty.
findings.signal_type_basis: Cyclical softening in services over weeks/months, linked to policy and geo risks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Early warning of demand weakness in dominant US growth sector.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] PMI data with analyst commentary on drivers.

sources_cited.excerpt: PMI... fell to 49.8... strain of rising prices and... tariffs

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Standard PMI metric with causal analysis provided.

findings.title: China 10Y bond yield falls amid global yield surge
findings.summary: Unlike US/EU/Japan, China's 10Y yield declined modestly since Middle East conflict start. Reflects domestic resilience or policy support amid global risk-off. Divergence highlights China safe-haven shift.
findings.claim_excerpt: the one exception is China, where the yield on the 10-year bond has fallen modestly since the conflict in the Middle East began.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term divergence in EM vs DM bonds amid crisis.
findings.preliminary_assessment: China decoupling from DM yield pressure, potential capital flow signal.
findings.confidence_basis: Single source observation without yield quantum.

search_queries_run: China bond yield Middle East conflict
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Assessed due to single source; needs multi-source yield data.

cross_monitor_relevance.scem: China bond strength signals EM resilience

findings.review_reason: Confirm yield trend direction and magnitude

findings.title: Eurozone inflation accelerates, ECB hike odds rise
findings.summary: Eurozone inflation picked up, with markets pricing ECB rate hikes 1-2 times by end-2026. Driven by core inflation acceleration expectations. Pressures DM bonds and policy divergence.
findings.claim_excerpt: investors are now expecting acceleration in core inflation... ECB will raise the benchmark interest rate one or two times before the end of 2026.
findings.signal_type_basis: Policy path shift over months from inflation momentum.
findings.preliminary_assessment: ECB tightening odds boost yield pressure, widens US-EU policy gap.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Futures market pricing cited directly.

findings.title: US CPI up 2.5% YoY in March, largest since Jan 2025
findings.summary: US consumer prices rose 2.5% YoY and 1.2% MoM in March, biggest gains in months. Fuels inflation fears contributing to bond yield spikes. Core pressures challenge soft landing narrative.
findings.claim_excerpt: Consumer prices were up 2.5% in March from a year earlier—the biggest increase since January 2025.
findings.signal_type_basis: Persistent inflation trend building over months.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Hot CPI reinforces bond selloff and policy tightening risks.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from Confirmed: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Specific CPI figures from weekly update.
